retrieved_docs.title: Drug Eluting Stent Compounds -- Issues of Concern -- The Coating
retrieved_docs.content: First generation DES used synthetic polymers such as poly(ethylene-co-vinyl acetate) (PEVA) and poly(n-butyl methacrylate) (PBMA) or tri-block copolymer poly(styrene-b-isobtylene-b-styrene) (SIBS). PEVA and PBMA coat SES whereas SIBS coats PES. SIBS allows early burst release of the drug which is ideal for controlling the early vascular injury following stent implantation. Observations of late stent thrombosis with PEVA and PBMA triggered investigations to find the underlying causes. Hypersensitivity arose as a factor as tissue surrounding stents became infiltrated by inflammatory cells. [76] To reduce inflammation following stenting, more biocompatible polymers such as phosphorylcholine (PC) and copolymer poly(vinylidene flouride-co-hexaflouropropylene) (PVDF-HFP) coat second-generation drug-eluting stents. Phosphorylcholine (PC) is a natural constituent of the phospholipid bilayer cell membrane. Since PC reduces platelet adhesion, it is useful to reduce thrombosis. Large vessel grafts are made from poly(bis(trifluoroethoxyl)phosphazene (PTFE).

retrieved_docs.title: Umbilical Hernia -- Pearls and Other Issues
retrieved_docs.content: Umbilical hernias are commonly repaired using synthetic, nonabsorbable mesh materials, such as polypropylene, polyethylene terephthalate polyester, or expanded polytetrafluoroethylene. [56] However, the choice of mesh depends on the anatomical placement and the presence of contamination or infection. Synthetic, slowly absorbable mesh is used to minimize postoperative adhesions and is preferred in infected or contaminated fields where nonabsorbable materials are contraindicated. Examples include, but are not limited to, polyglycolic acid with trimethylene carbonate, polyglactin, and poly-4-hydroxybutyrate. [57] These mesh materials typically degrade within 1 to 3 months and are associated with higher rates of recurrence. [29]

retrieved_docs.title: Minimally Invasive Glaucoma Surgery -- Technique or Treatment -- Xen Implant (Xen Gel Stent)
retrieved_docs.content: This hydrophilic tube is made up of porcine collagen-derived gelatin, which is crosslinked with glutaraldehyde. The device is in a rigid state and straight when dry and mouldable when soft and hydrated. This is 6 mm in length and has three types XEN 140, XEN 63, and XEN 45. XEN 45 is FDA-approved with an inner lumen of 45 um and an outer diameter of 150 um. The device has the advantage that it prevents the risk of hypotony. This is available as a pre-loaded device with a disposable 27G injector inserted in the anterior chamber through an ab interno approach. [39]

retrieved_docs.title: Amoxapine -- Indications -- Off-Label Uses
retrieved_docs.content: Research conducted in mice has shown that amoxapine can reduce the production of amyloid-beta chains associated with Alzheimer disease by targeting the serotonin-6 (HTR-6, 5-HT-6) receptor. [3] Multiple studies have shown that amoxapine can reduce the incidence of diarrhea in patients undergoing chemotherapy, particularly when used in conjunction with irinotecan. [4] [5] Furthermore, research has highlighted amoxapine's effectiveness in the management of neuropathic pain. [6]

retrieved_docs.title: Hydroxyapatite Dental Material -- Issues of Concern
retrieved_docs.content: The applications of hydroxyapatite in hard tissue restoration and drug delivery do not typically involve its pure form. Pure hydroxyapatite has relatively low mechanical properties and is brittle, making it unsuitable for load-bearing applications. Consequently, hydroxyapatite is often incorporated into composites or polymers to enhance its functionality. [11] [16] In this case, the enhanced properties of hydroxyapatite result from the compressive strength of the hydroxyapatite ceramic phase, combined with the toughness and elasticity of the polymer or composite matrix. Generally, hydroxyapatite exhibits resistance to resorption in vivo at a rate of 1% to 2% per year, providing long-lasting structural support in the defect area. [8] [12]

retrieved_docs.title: Trinucleotide Repeat Disorders -- Pearls and Other Issues
retrieved_docs.content: Innate trophic support from MSC or augmented growth factor support, such as delivering brain-derived neurotrophic factor or glial-derived neurotrophic factor, is in clinical trial phases II and III for treating trinucleotide repeat disorder.

retrieved_docs.title: Ventral Hernia -- Treatment / Management
retrieved_docs.content: Mesh can be divided into synthetic or biologic. The decision of which mesh to use is mainly up to the surgeon, but there are instances where one should be used over the other. Most synthetic prosthetic grafts can be categorized as derived from polypropylene, polyester, or polytetrafluoroethylene (PTFE). In the late 1990s, lightweight mesh came in the market and has now been widely accepted as a superior mesh, but it still can become infected and have recurrences. The size of the pores in the mesh has also come under scrutiny and they have discovered there is a major benefit to macro-porous mesh, defined as pores larger than 3 mm. These cause fewer incidences of infection, and if the do become infected, there is a much higher chance that treatment with antibiotics can prevent explantation. Mesh composed of ePTFE has a good profile for adhesion risk but a high risk of infection. In contrast, polypropylene mesh has a lower infection risk but little flexibility and a high adhesion risk. There is also 2-sided mesh designed for intra-peritoneal placement in which one side is coated with an anti-adhesive barrier. This is not 100% successful, but studies have shown decreased adhesion formations and easier adhesiolysis of those that do form. There is also sutureless mesh that sticks to the tissues designed to reduce post-operative pain and prevent mesh migration. Absorbable mesh-like vicryl can be used in infected fields, but it will absorb over time leaving only native tissue.

Advantages: Good elastic recovery Pleasant odor and taste Accurate reproduction 6. Disadvantages: Dimensional deformation over time Hydrophobic Dimensional stability is affected due to reaction byproducts [25] Delivery Systems of Elastomers

retrieved_docs.title: Viscoelastics -- Anatomy and Physiology -- Cohesiveness
retrieved_docs.content: Cohesiveness is a property by which molecules of viscoelastics adhere to one another and form a clump. Higher the molecular weight, higher the surface tension, and higher the pseudo-plasticity, higher is the cohesiveness of a viscoelastic. Sodium hyaluronate is more cohesive than HPMC and is challenging to aspirate from the anterior chamber during surgery. [10]

retrieved_docs.title: Amoxapine -- Mechanism of Action
retrieved_docs.content: Amoxapine, a second-generation tricyclic dibenzoxazepine antidepressant, primarily functions by inhibiting the reuptake of norepinephrine in neuronal synapses. The drug exhibits minimal effect on the histamine H1 receptor and serotonin receptors, except for the serotonin-6 receptor (HTR-6, 5-HT-6). [7] [8] [9] Furthermore, amoxapine also acts as a dopamine (DA) receptor antagonist, specifically targeting D2 and D4 receptors. [10]

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 26 -- Level 3 -- Community 2062
retrieved_docs.content: CAESAREAN SECTION SCAR TYPE UNKNOWN is clinically associated with both الانفصال المبكر للمشيمة (early placental separation) and مخاض قبل الأوان (preterm labor). In addition, REPRODUCTIVE HISTORY POOR is clinically associated with مخاض قبل الأوان, indicating that an unknown‑type cesarean section scar and a poor reproductive history are both risk factors for preterm labor, while the unknown‑type scar also predisposes to early placental separation.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 6 -- Level 3 -- Community 1494
retrieved_docs.content: A gestational age of 29‑30 weeks completed is associated with a liveborn infant, indicating that pregnancies reaching this stage can result in a live birth.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 28 -- Level 3 -- Community 1697
retrieved_docs.content: A caesarean section scar of unknown type and a poor reproductive history are clinically associated with مخاض قبل الأوان (preterm labor), indicating that women who have an indeterminate type of previous cesarean scar or who have experienced suboptimal reproductive outcomes are at increased risk for premature labor. Recognizing these associations enables clinicians to identify high‑risk patients, implement closer surveillance, and consider preventive strategies to reduce the likelihood of preterm delivery.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 9 -- Level 3 -- Community 2316
retrieved_docs.content: A poor reproductive history, reflecting previous obstetric complications or adverse outcomes, is clinically associated with an increased risk of preterm labor (مخاض قبل الأوان). This connection highlights the importance of detailed reproductive assessments in prenatal care to identify women who may be more likely to experience early delivery and to apply targeted monitoring and preventive strategies.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 3 -- Community 2304
retrieved_docs.content: Early, first or unspecified trimester hemorrhage is classified as spotting complicating pregnancy, unspecified trimester.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 3 -- Community 2324
retrieved_docs.content: Induction by cervical dilation and other procedures to assist delivery are closely related interventions used to facilitate childbirth, and they may be considered synonymous in clinical practice.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 2 -- Community 997
retrieved_docs.content: Intervillous hemorrhage of placenta is a disorder characterized by bleeding into the placental intervillous space and is regarded as a condition affecting the fetal membranes or amniotic fluid; it occurs in the context of a pregnancy in which a child is present.

Recognizing these interrelationships supports more precise risk stratification, diagnosis, and management of pregnant patients across the full spectrum of obstetric care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 17 -- Level 2 -- Community 552

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 2 -- Community 898
retrieved_docs.content: temporal occurrence during the fetal period, providing a detailed framework for understanding how diverse maternal and fetal conditions can impact labor and delivery outcomes and guiding clinicians in assessing and managing these complex scenarios.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 4 -- Community 2791
retrieved_docs.content: Episiotomy and تمزق الأغشية الاصطناعي (artificial membrane rupture) are both classified under الإجراءات العلاجية الخاصة بالتوليد (obstetric therapeutic procedures). By recognizing these interventions as subcomponents of the broader category of obstetric therapeutic care, clinicians can better organize treatment options, understand their related indications, and apply appropriate management strategies within the field of obstetrics.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 17 -- Level 4 -- Community 2820
retrieved_docs.content: Cerclage (procedure) is clinically associated with قصور عنق الرحم, a condition in which the uterine cervix is unable to maintain sufficient closure during pregnancy, leading to an increased risk of preterm birth or pregnancy loss. The cerclage technique involves placing a suturing material around the cervix to reinforce its structural integrity, thereby preventing premature cervical dilation. This intervention is employed as a therapeutic measure to manage cervical insufficiency and improve obstetric outcomes for affected patients.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 6 -- Level 3 -- Community 1447
retrieved_docs.content: Cerclage (procedure) and REPRODUCTIVE HISTORY POOR are both clinically associated with قصور عنق الرحم. قصور عنق الرحم, also known as cervical insufficiency, is a condition in which the cervix is unable to maintain its structural integrity during pregnancy, increasing the risk of premature dilation and preterm delivery. The cerclage procedure involves placing a suturing stitch around the cervix to reinforce it and prevent premature opening, thereby directly addressing the mechanical weakness characteristic of قصور عنق الرحم. Additionally, a poor reproductive history—such as prior miscarriages, preterm births, or other adverse pregnancy outcomes—has been linked to a higher likelihood of developing قصور عنق الرحم, indicating that past obstetric complications can serve as a clinical indicator for this cervical disorder. Recognizing both the therapeutic role of cerclage and the predictive value of reproductive history is essential for early identification and management of patients at risk for قصور عنق الرحم.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 3 -- Level 1 -- Community 102
retrieved_docs.content: and cardiovascular complications are a subset of the overall perinatal condition spectrum. Collectively, these interrelated entities illustrate how gestational age, birthweight, and fetal growth trajectories define a network of classifications—from specific gestational milestones (29‑30 weeks) to broad perinatal disorder groups—providing a comprehensive framework for understanding, diagnosing, and managing health issues in liveborn infants.
